   589	    class Meta:
   590	        ordering = ('cable', 'cable_end', 'connector', 'pk')

   592	            models.UniqueConstraint(
   593	                fields=('termination_type', 'termination_id'),
   594	                name='%(app_label)s_%(class)s_unique_termination'
   595	            ),
   596	            models.UniqueConstraint(
   597	                fields=('cable', 'cable_end', 'connector'),
   598	                name='%(app_label)s_%(class)s_unique_connector'
   599	            ),
   600	        )
   601	        verbose_name = _('cable termination')
   602	        verbose_name_plural = _('cable terminations')
   603	
   604	    def __str__(self):
   605	        return f'Cable {self.cable} to {self.termination}'
   606	
   607	    def clean(self):
   608	        super().clean()
   609	
   610	        # Disallow connecting a cable to any termination object that is
   611	        # explicitly flagged as "mark connected".
   612	        termination = getattr(self, 'termination', None)
   613	        if termination is not None and getattr(termination, "mark_connected", False):
   614	            raise ValidationError(
   615	                _("Cannot connect a cable to {obj_parent} > {obj} because it is marked as connected.").format(
   616	                    obj_parent=termination.parent_object,
   617	                    obj=termination,
   618	                )
   619	            )
   620	
   621	        # Check for existing termination
   622	        qs = CableTermination.objects.filter(
   623	            termination_type=self.termination_type,
   624	            termination_id=self.termination_id
   625	        )
   626	        if self.cable.pk:
   627	            qs = qs.exclude(cable=self.cable)
   628	
   629	        existing_termination = qs.first()
   630	        if existing_termination is not None:
   631	            raise ValidationError(
   632	                _("Duplicate termination found for {app_label}.{model} {termination_id}: cable {cable_pk}").format(
   633	                    app_label=self.termination_type.app_label,
   634	                    model=self.termination_type.model,
   635	                    termination_id=self.termination_id,
   636	                    cable_pk=existing_termination.cable.pk
   637	                )
   638	            )
   639	        # Validate the interface type (if applicable)
   640	        if self.termination_type.model == 'interface' and self.termination.type in NONCONNECTABLE_IFACE_TYPES:
   641	            raise ValidationError(
   642	                _("Cables cannot be terminated to {type_display} interfaces").format(
   643	                    type_display=self.termination.get_type_display()
   644	                )
   645	            )
   646	
   647	        # A CircuitTermination attached to a ProviderNetwork cannot have a Cable
   648	        if self.termination_type.model == 'circuittermination' and self.termination._provider_network is not None:
   649	            raise ValidationError(_("Circuit terminations attached to a provider network may not be cabled."))
   650	
   651	    def save(self, *args, **kwargs):
   652	
   653	        # Cache objects associated with the terminating object (for filtering)
   654	        self.cache_related_objects()
   655	
   656	        super().save(*args, **kwargs)
   657	
   658	        # Set the cable on the terminating object
   659	        termination = self.termination._meta.model.objects.get(pk=self.termination_id)
   660	        termination.snapshot()
   661	        termination.set_cable_termination(self)
   662	        termination.save()
   663	
   664	    def delete(self, *args, **kwargs):
   665	
   666	        # Delete the cable association on the terminating object
   667	        termination = self.termination._meta.model.objects.get(pk=self.termination_id)
   668	        termination.snapshot()
   669	        termination.clear_cable_termination(self)
   670	        termination.save()
   671	
   672	        super().delete(*args, **kwargs)
   673	
   674	    def cache_related_objects(self):
   675	        """
   676	        Cache objects related to the termination (e.g. device, rack, site) directly on the object to
   677	        enable efficient filtering.
   678	        """
   679	        if self.termination is None:
   680	            raise ValueError(
   681	                _("Invalid cable termination: the assigned termination object does not exist.")
   682	            )
   683	
   684	        # Device components
   685	        if getattr(self.termination, 'device', None):
   686	            self._device = self.termination.device
   687	            self._rack = self.termination.device.rack
   688	            self._location = self.termination.device.location
   689	            self._site = self.termination.device.site
   690	
   691	        # Power feeds
   692	        elif getattr(self.termination, 'rack', None):
   693	            self._rack = self.termination.rack
   694	            self._location = self.termination.rack.location
   695	            self._site = self.termination.rack.site
   696	
   697	        # Circuit terminations (which cache their own site/location)
   698	        elif self.termination._meta.label_lower == 'circuits.circuittermination':
   699	            self._site = self.termination._site
   700	            self._location = self.termination._location
   701	    cache_related_objects.alters_data = True
   702	
   703	    def to_objectchange(self, action):
   704	        objectchange = super().to_objectchange(action)
   705	        objectchange.related_object = self.termination
   706	        return objectchange

   781	        if self.label:
   782	            return self.label
   783	        if self.device_type and self.asset_tag:

   785	        if self.device_type:

   789	    def clean(self):
   790	        super().clean()
   791	
   792	        # Validate site/location/rack combination
   793	        if self.rack and self.site != self.rack.site:
   794	            raise ValidationError({
   795	                'rack': _("Rack {rack} does not belong to site {site}.").format(rack=self.rack, site=self.site),
   796	            })
   797	        if self.location and self.site != self.location.site:
   798	            raise ValidationError({
   799	                'location': _(
   800	                    "Location {location} does not belong to site {site}."
   801	                ).format(location=self.location, site=self.site)
   802	            })
   803	        if self.rack and self.location and self.rack.location != self.location:
   804	            raise ValidationError({
   805	                'rack': _(
   806	                    "Rack {rack} does not belong to location {location}."
   807	                ).format(rack=self.rack, location=self.location)
   808	            })
   809	
   810	        if self.rack is None:
   811	            if self.face:
   812	                raise ValidationError({
   813	                    'face': _("Cannot select a rack face without assigning a rack."),
   814	                })
   815	            if self.position:
   816	                raise ValidationError({
   817	                    'position': _("Cannot select a rack position without assigning a rack."),
   818	                })
   819	
   820	        # Validate rack position and face
   821	        if self.position and self.position % decimal.Decimal(0.5):
   822	            raise ValidationError({
   823	                'position': _("Position must be in increments of 0.5 rack units.")
   824	            })
   825	        if self.position and not self.face:
   826	            raise ValidationError({
   827	                'face': _("Must specify rack face when defining rack position."),
   828	            })
   829	
   830	        # Prevent 0U devices from being assigned to a specific position
   831	        if hasattr(self, 'device_type'):
   832	            if self.position and self.device_type.u_height == 0:
   833	                raise ValidationError({
   834	                    'position': _(
   835	                        "A 0U device type ({device_type}) cannot be assigned to a rack position."
   836	                    ).format(device_type=self.device_type)
   837	                })
   838	
   839	        if self.rack:
   840	
   841	            try:
   842	                # Child devices cannot be assigned to a rack face/unit
   843	                if self.device_type.is_child_device and self.face:
   844	                    raise ValidationError({
   845	                        'face': _(
   846	                            "Child device types cannot be assigned to a rack face. This is an attribute of the parent "
   847	                            "device."
   848	                        )
   849	                    })
   850	                if self.device_type.is_child_device and self.position:
   851	                    raise ValidationError({
   852	                        'position': _(
   853	                            "Child device types cannot be assigned to a rack position. This is an attribute of the "
   854	                            "parent device."
   855	                        )
   856	                    })
   857	
   858	                # Validate rack space
   859	                rack_face = self.face if not self.device_type.is_full_depth else None
   860	                exclude_list = [self.pk] if self.pk else []
   861	                available_units = self.rack.get_available_units(
   862	                    u_height=self.device_type.u_height, rack_face=rack_face, exclude=exclude_list
   863	                )
   864	                if self.position and self.position not in available_units:
   865	                    raise ValidationError({
   866	                        'position': _(
   867	                            "U{position} is already occupied or does not have sufficient space to accommodate this "
   868	                            "device type: {device_type} ({u_height}U)"
   869	                        ).format(
   870	                            position=self.position, device_type=self.device_type, u_height=self.device_type.u_height
   871	                        )
   872	                    })
   873	
   874	            except DeviceType.DoesNotExist:
   875	                pass
   876	
   877	        # Validate primary & OOB IP addresses
   878	        vc_interfaces = self.vc_interfaces(if_master=False)
   879	        if self.primary_ip4:
   880	            if self.primary_ip4.family != 4:
   881	                raise ValidationError({
   882	                    'primary_ip4': _("{ip} is not an IPv4 address.").format(ip=self.primary_ip4)
   883	                })
   884	            if self.primary_ip4.assigned_object in vc_interfaces:
   885	                pass
   886	            elif (
   887	                    self.primary_ip4.nat_inside is not None and
   888	                    self.primary_ip4.nat_inside.assigned_object in vc_interfaces
   889	            ):
   890	                pass
   891	            else:
   892	                raise ValidationError({
   893	                    'primary_ip4': _(
   894	                        "The specified IP address ({ip}) is not assigned to this device."
   895	                    ).format(ip=self.primary_ip4)
   896	                })
   897	        if self.primary_ip6:
   898	            if self.primary_ip6.family != 6:
   899	                raise ValidationError({
   900	                    'primary_ip6': _("{ip} is not an IPv6 address.").format(ip=self.primary_ip6)
   901	                })
   902	            if self.primary_ip6.assigned_object in vc_interfaces:
   903	                pass
   904	            elif (
   905	                    self.primary_ip6.nat_inside is not None and
   906	                    self.primary_ip6.nat_inside.assigned_object in vc_interfaces
   907	            ):
   908	                pass
   909	            else:
   910	                raise ValidationError({
   911	                    'primary_ip6': _(
   912	                        "The specified IP address ({ip}) is not assigned to this device."
   913	                    ).format(ip=self.primary_ip6)
   914	                })
   915	        if self.oob_ip:
   916	            if self.oob_ip.assigned_object in vc_interfaces:
   917	                pass
   918	            elif self.oob_ip.nat_inside is not None and self.oob_ip.nat_inside.assigned_object in vc_interfaces:
   919	                pass
   920	            else:
   921	                raise ValidationError({
   922	                    'oob_ip': f"The specified IP address ({self.oob_ip}) is not assigned to this device."
   923	                })
   924	
   925	        # Validate manufacturer/platform
   926	        if hasattr(self, 'device_type') and self.platform:
   927	            if self.platform.manufacturer and self.platform.manufacturer != self.device_type.manufacturer:
   928	                raise ValidationError({
   929	                    'platform': _(
   930	                        "The assigned platform is limited to {platform_manufacturer} device types, but this device's "
   931	                        "type belongs to {devicetype_manufacturer}."
   932	                    ).format(

   935	                    )
   936	                })
   937	
   938	        # A Device can only be assigned to a Cluster in the same Site (or no Site)

   981	    def _instantiate_components(self, queryset, bulk_create=True):
   982	        """
   983	        Instantiate components for the device from the specified component templates.
   984	
   985	        Args:

   987	                         (default). Otherwise, save() will be called on each instance individually.

   990	
   991	        if bulk_create:
   992	            components = [obj.instantiate(device=self) for obj in queryset]
   993	            if not components:
   994	                return
   995	
   996	            # Check for duplicate names after resolution {vc_position}
   997	            self._check_duplicate_component_names(components)

   999	            # Set default values for any applicable custom fields
  1000	            if cf_defaults := CustomField.objects.get_defaults_for_model(model):
  1001	                for component in components:
  1002	                    component.custom_field_data = cf_defaults

  1005	                component._site = self.site
  1006	                component._location = self.location

  1016	                    instance=component,
  1017	                    created=True,
  1018	                    raw=False,
  1019	                    using='default',

  1024	            if not components:

  1026	
  1027	            # Check for duplicate names after resolution {vc_position}
  1028	            self._check_duplicate_component_names(components)
  1029	
  1030	            for component in components:

  1048	        if self.rack and self.rack.location:

  1054	        if is_new:
  1055	            self._instantiate_components(self.device_type.consoleporttemplates.all())
  1056	            self._instantiate_components(self.device_type.consoleserverporttemplates.all())
  1057	            self._instantiate_components(self.device_type.powerporttemplates.all())
  1058	            self._instantiate_components(self.device_type.poweroutlettemplates.all())
  1059	            self._instantiate_components(self.device_type.interfacetemplates.all())
  1060	            self._instantiate_components(self.device_type.rearporttemplates.all())
  1061	            self._instantiate_components(self.device_type.frontporttemplates.all())
  1062	            # Replicate any front/rear port mappings from the DeviceType
  1063	            create_port_mappings(self, self.device_type)

  1066	            self._instantiate_components(self.device_type.devicebaytemplates.all())
  1067	            # Disable bulk_create to accommodate MPTT
  1068	            self._instantiate_components(self.device_type.inventoryitemtemplates.all(), bulk_create=False)
  1069	            # Interface bridges have to be set after interface instantiation
  1070	            update_interface_bridges(self, self.device_type.interfacetemplates.all())
  1071	
  1072	        # Update Site and Rack assignment for any child Devices
  1073	        devices = Device.objects.filter(parent_bay__device=self)
  1074	        for device in devices:
  1075	            device.site = self.site
  1076	            device.rack = self.rack

  1134	        from .cables import Cable

   256	    def link_peers(self):

   265	    def _get_profile_link_peers(self):

   267	            return []
   268	
   269	        profile = self.cable.profile_class()
   270	        peer_terminations = {
   271	            (peer.connector, position): peer.termination
   272	            for peer in self.cable.terminations.all()
   273	            if peer.cable_end == self.opposite_cable_end and peer.connector is not None
   274	            for position in peer.positions or []

   280	            if mapped_position is None:
   281	                continue
   282	
   283	            peer = peer_terminations.get(mapped_position)
   284	            if peer is not None and peer not in link_peers:
   285	                link_peers.append(peer)

   322	class PathEndpoint(models.Model):
   323	    """
   324	    An abstract model inherited by any CabledObjectModel subclass which represents the end of a CablePath; specifically,
   325	    these include ConsolePort, ConsoleServerPort, PowerPort, PowerOutlet, Interface, and PowerFeed.
   326	
   327	    `_path` references the CablePath originating from this instance, if any. It is set or cleared by the receivers in
   328	    dcim.signals in response to changes in the cable path, and complements the `origin` GenericForeignKey field on the
   329	    CablePath model. `_path` should not be accessed directly; rather, use the `path` property.
   330	
   331	    `connected_endpoints()` is a convenience method for returning the destination of the associated CablePath, if any.
   332	    """
   333	
   334	    _path = models.ForeignKey(
   335	        to='dcim.CablePath',
   336	        on_delete=models.SET_NULL,
   337	        null=True,
   338	        blank=True,
   339	    )
   340	
   341	    class Meta:
   342	        abstract = True
   343	
   344	    def trace(self):
   345	        origin = self
   346	        path = []
   347	
   348	        # Construct the complete path (including e.g. bridged interfaces)
   349	        while origin is not None:
   350	            # Go through the public accessor rather than dereferencing `_path`
   351	            # directly. During cable edits, CablePath rows can be deleted and
   352	            # recreated while this endpoint instance is still in memory.
   353	            cable_path = origin.path
   354	            if cable_path is None:
   355	                break
   356	
   357	            path.extend(cable_path.path_objects)
   358	
   359	            # If the path ends at a non-connected pass-through port, pad out the link and far-end terminations
   360	            if len(path) % 3 == 1:
   361	                path.extend(([], []))
   362	            # If the path ends at a site or provider network, inject a null "link" to render an attachment
   363	            elif len(path) % 3 == 2:
   364	                path.insert(-1, [])
   365	
   366	            # Check for a bridged relationship to continue the trace.
   367	            destinations = cable_path.destinations
   368	            if len(destinations) == 1:
   369	                origin = getattr(destinations[0], 'bridge', None)
   370	            else:
   371	                origin = None
   372	
   373	        # Return the path as a list of three-tuples (A termination(s), cable(s), B termination(s))
   374	        return list(zip(*[iter(path)] * 3))
   375	
   376	    @property
   377	    def path(self):
   378	        """
   379	        Return this endpoint's current CablePath, if any.
   380	
   381	        `_path` is a denormalized reference that is updated from CablePath
   382	        save/delete handlers, including queryset.update() calls on origin
   383	        endpoints. That means an already-instantiated endpoint can briefly hold
   384	        a stale in-memory `_path` relation while the database already points to
   385	        a different CablePath (or to no path at all).
   386	
   387	        Two stale cases are repaired by refreshing only the `_path` field
   388	        from the database:
   389	
   390	        1. The endpoint is linked (by cable or wireless link) but `_path` is
   391	           unset, because the instance was loaded before its path was traced
   392	           (e.g. while queued for event serialization during link creation).
   393	        2. The cached relation points to a CablePath row that has just been
   394	           deleted.
   395	
   396	        Repairing case 1 costs one query per access for a linked endpoint
   397	        whose path is genuinely absent in the database. That state is
   398	        transient outside of tracing failures, so no result caching is
   399	        attempted here.
   400	        """
   401	        if self._path_id is None:
   402	            has_link = self.cable_id is not None or getattr(self, 'wireless_link_id', None) is not None
   403	            if self.pk and has_link:
   404	                self.refresh_from_db(fields=['_path'])
   405	
   406	            if self._path_id is None:
   407	                return None
   408	
   409	        try:
   410	            return self._path
   411	        except ObjectDoesNotExist:
   412	            # Refresh only the denormalized FK instead of the whole model.
   413	            # The expected problem here is in-memory staleness during path
   414	            # rebuilds, not persistent database corruption.
   415	            self.refresh_from_db(fields=['_path'])
   416	            return self._path if self._path_id else None
   417	
   418	    @cached_property
   419	    def connected_endpoints(self):
   420	        """
   421	        Caching accessor for the attached CablePath's destinations (if any).
   422	
   423	        Always route through `path` so stale in-memory `_path` references are
   424	        repaired before we cache the result for the lifetime of this instance.
   425	        """
   426	        if cable_path := self.path:
   427	            return cable_path.destinations
   428	        return []

   510	        help_text=_("Maximum power draw (watts)")
   511	    )

   522	    class Meta(ModularComponentModel.Meta):
   523	        verbose_name = _('power port')
   524	        verbose_name_plural = _('power ports')
   525	
   526	    def clean(self):
   527	        super().clean()
   528	
   529	        if self.maximum_draw is not None and self.allocated_draw is not None:
   530	            if self.allocated_draw > self.maximum_draw:
   531	                raise ValidationError({
   532	                    'allocated_draw': _(
   533	                        "Allocated draw cannot exceed the maximum draw ({maximum_draw}W)."
   534	                    ).format(maximum_draw=self.maximum_draw)
   535	                })
   536	
   537	    def get_downstream_powerports(self, leg=None):
   538	        """
   539	        Return a queryset of all PowerPorts connected via cable to a child PowerOutlet. For example, in the topology
   540	        below, PP1.get_downstream_powerports() would return PP2-4.
   541	
   542	               ---- PO1 <---> PP2
   543	             /
   544	        PP1 ------- PO2 <---> PP3
   545	             \
   546	               ---- PO3 <---> PP4
   547	
   548	        """
   549	        poweroutlets = self.poweroutlets.filter(cable__isnull=False)
   550	        if leg:
   551	            poweroutlets = poweroutlets.filter(feed_leg=leg)
   552	        if not poweroutlets:
   553	            return PowerPort.objects.none()
   554	
   555	        q = Q()
   556	        for poweroutlet in poweroutlets:
   557	            q |= Q(
   558	                cable=poweroutlet.cable,
   559	                cable_end=poweroutlet.opposite_cable_end
   560	            )
   561	
   562	        return PowerPort.objects.filter(q)
   563	
   564	    def get_power_draw(self, _seen=None):
   565	        """
   566	        Return the allocated and maximum power draw (in VA) and child PowerOutlet count for this PowerPort.
   567	        """
   568	        from dcim.models import PowerFeed
   569	
   570	        # Calculate aggregate draw of all child power outlets if no numbers have been defined manually
   571	        if self.allocated_draw is None and self.maximum_draw is None:
   572	
   573	            def _aggregate(powerports, seen):
   574	                # Recursively resolve the draw for each downstream PowerPort. Using the per-port value
   575	                # (rather than a SQL aggregate over allocated_draw/maximum_draw) allows the draw to
   576	                # propagate through intermediate auto-mode PowerPorts, e.g. PDU-internal fuse chains.
   577	                # `seen` tracks visited PowerPorts to prevent infinite recursion if the topology
   578	                # happens to form a cycle.
   579	                allocated_total = 0
   580	                maximum_total = 0
   581	                for powerport in powerports:
   582	                    if powerport.pk in seen:
   583	                        continue
   584	                    seen.add(powerport.pk)
   585	                    draw = powerport.get_power_draw(_seen=seen)
   586	                    allocated_total += draw['allocated']
   587	                    maximum_total += draw['maximum']
   588	                return allocated_total, maximum_total
   589	
   590	            # Seed each _aggregate() call with a fresh copy of the inherited visited set so the full
   591	            # and per-leg aggregations are independent. Otherwise, ports visited during the full
   592	            # aggregation would be skipped during the per-leg passes.
   593	            base_seen = set(_seen) if _seen else set()
   594	            base_seen.add(self.pk)
   595	
   596	            allocated, maximum = _aggregate(self.get_downstream_powerports(), set(base_seen))
   597	            ret = {
   598	                'allocated': allocated,
   599	                'maximum': maximum,
   600	                'outlet_count': self.poweroutlets.count(),
   601	                'legs': [],
   602	            }
   603	
   604	            # Calculate per-leg aggregates for three-phase power feeds
   605	            if len(self.link_peers) == 1 and isinstance(self.link_peers[0], PowerFeed) and \
   606	                    self.link_peers[0].phase == PowerFeedPhaseChoices.PHASE_3PHASE:
   607	                for leg, leg_name in PowerOutletFeedLegChoices:
   608	                    leg_allocated, leg_maximum = _aggregate(
   609	                        self.get_downstream_powerports(leg=leg), set(base_seen)
   610	                    )
   611	                    ret['legs'].append({
   612	                        'name': leg_name,
   613	                        'allocated': leg_allocated,
   614	                        'maximum': leg_maximum,
   615	                        'outlet_count': self.poweroutlets.filter(feed_leg=leg).count(),
   616	                    })
   617	
   618	            return ret
   619	
   620	        # Default to administratively defined values
   621	        return {
   622	            'allocated': self.allocated_draw or 0,
   623	            'maximum': self.maximum_draw or 0,
   624	            'outlet_count': self.poweroutlets.count(),
   625	            'legs': [],
   626	        }

  1331	class ModuleBayManager(TreeManager):
  1332	    """
  1333	    Order ModuleBays by the natural-sort name of each tree's root, then by MPTT
  1334	    left value within the tree. Combined with the root-insert bypass in
  1335	    ModuleBay.save(), this lets us keep MPTTMeta.order_insertion_by for cheap
  1336	    intra-tree sibling placement while skipping the global tree_id renumbering
  1337	    it would otherwise perform on every root insert.
  1338	    """
  1339	    def get_queryset(self, *args, **kwargs):
  1340	        # Use the raw manager to avoid recursing through this get_queryset() when
  1341	        # building the annotation subquery.
  1342	        root_name = self.model._objects_raw.filter(
  1343	            tree_id=models.OuterRef('tree_id'),
  1344	            level=0,
  1345	        ).values('name')[:1]
  1346	        return super().get_queryset(*args, **kwargs).annotate(
  1347	            _root_name=models.Subquery(
  1348	                root_name,
  1349	                output_field=models.CharField(db_collation='natural_sort'),
  1350	            )
  1351	        ).order_by('_root_name', 'lft')

  1357	    """

  1380	    # without recursing through our annotated get_queryset().

  1385	    class Meta(ModularComponentModel.Meta):
  1386	        # Empty tuple triggers Django migration detection for MPTT indexes
  1387	        # (see #21016, django-mptt/django-mptt#682)
  1388	        indexes = ()
  1389	        constraints = (
  1390	            models.UniqueConstraint(
  1391	                fields=('device', 'module', 'name'),
  1392	                name='%(app_label)s_%(class)s_unique_device_module_name'
  1393	            ),
  1394	        )
  1395	        verbose_name = _('module bay')
  1396	        verbose_name_plural = _('module bays')
  1397	
  1398	    class MPTTMeta:
  1399	        # Used for placing siblings within a single tree at insert time. Costs
  1400	        # are bounded to that tree's rows. Cross-tree (root) insertion is
  1401	        # handled in save() to avoid the O(N) tree_id shift this would trigger.
  1402	        order_insertion_by = ('name',)
  1403	
  1404	    def clean(self):
  1405	        super().clean()
  1406	
  1407	        # Check for recursion
  1408	        if module := self.module:
  1409	            module_bays = [self.pk]
  1410	            modules = []
  1411	            while module:
  1412	                if module.pk in modules or module.module_bay.pk in module_bays:
  1413	                    raise ValidationError(_("A module bay cannot belong to a module installed within it."))
  1414	                modules.append(module.pk)
  1415	                module_bays.append(module.module_bay.pk)
  1416	                module = module.module_bay.module if module.module_bay else None
  1417	
  1418	    def save(self, *args, **kwargs):
  1419	        if self.module:
  1420	            self.parent = self.module.module_bay
  1421	        else:
  1422	            self.parent = None
  1423	        opts = self._mptt_meta
  1424	        # For new root nodes, allocate the next tree_id and pre-set MPTT fields
  1425	        # so super().save() skips MPTT's order_insertion_by-driven insertion
  1426	        # path. That path would otherwise UPDATE every later tree_id on each
  1427	        # root insert (NB-2800). Children still go through MPTT, which keeps
  1428	        # siblings in name order via the same order_insertion_by setting.
  1429	        if self._state.adding and self.parent_id is None and not self.lft and not self.rght:
  1430	            max_tree_id = ModuleBay._objects_raw.aggregate(
  1431	                models.Max('tree_id')
  1432	            )['tree_id__max'] or 0
  1433	            self.tree_id = max_tree_id + 1
  1434	            self.lft = 1
  1435	            self.rght = 2
  1436	            self.level = 0
  1437	        elif (
  1438	            not self._state.adding
  1439	            and self.parent_id is None
  1440	            and self._mptt_cached_fields.get(opts.parent_attr) is None
  1441	        ):
  1442	            # Existing root being updated. Spoof the cached order_insertion_by
  1443	            # values so MPTT's same_order check passes and it skips its reorder
  1444	            # path, which would UPDATE every later tree_id on each root rename.
  1445	            # ModuleBayManager._root_name recovers display order at query time,
  1446	            # so the tree_id reshuffling would be wasted work. Child renames
  1447	            # and root<->child transitions intentionally fall through to MPTT.
  1448	            for field_name in opts.order_insertion_by:
  1449	                field_name = field_name.lstrip('-')
  1450	                self._mptt_cached_fields[field_name] = opts.get_raw_field_value(
  1451	                    self, field_name
  1452	                )
  1453	        super().save(*args, **kwargs)
  1454	
  1455	    @property
  1456	    def _occupied(self):
  1457	        """
  1458	        Indicates whether the module bay is occupied by a module.
  1459	        """
  1460	        return bool(not self.enabled or hasattr(self, 'installed_module'))

  1640	        # An InventoryItem cannot be its own parent

  1644	            })
  1645	
  1646	        # Validation for moving InventoryItems
  1647	        if not self._state.adding:
  1648	            # Cannot move an InventoryItem to another device if it has a parent
  1649	            if self.parent and self.parent.device != self.device:
  1650	                raise ValidationError({
  1651	                    "parent": _("Parent inventory item does not belong to the same device.")
  1652	                })
  1653	
  1654	            # Prevent moving InventoryItems with children
  1655	            first_child = self.get_children().first()
  1656	            if first_child and first_child.device != self.device:
  1657	                raise ValidationError(_("Cannot move an inventory item with dependent children"))
  1658	
  1659	            # When moving an InventoryItem to another device, remove any associated component
  1660	            if self.component and self.component.device != self.device:
  1661	                self.component = None
  1662	        else:
  1663	            if self.component and self.component.device != self.device:
  1664	                raise ValidationError({
  1665	                    "device": _("Cannot assign inventory item to component on another device")
  1666	                })

248	class Module(TrackingModelMixin, PrimaryModel):

   289	
   290	    class Meta:
   291	        ordering = ('module_bay',)
   292	        verbose_name = _('module')
   293	        verbose_name_plural = _('modules')
   294	
   295	    def __str__(self):
   296	        return f'{self.module_bay.name}: {self.module_type} ({self.pk})'
   297	
   298	    def get_status_color(self):
   299	        return ModuleStatusChoices.colors.get(self.status)
   300	
   301	    def clean(self):
   302	        super().clean()
   303	
   304	        if hasattr(self, "module_bay") and (self.module_bay.device != self.device):
   305	            raise ValidationError(
   306	                _("Module must be installed within a module bay belonging to the assigned device ({device}).").format(
   307	                    device=self.device
   308	                )
   309	            )
   310	
   311	        # Prevent module from being installed in a disabled bay
   312	        if hasattr(self, 'module_bay') and self.module_bay and not self.module_bay.enabled:
   313	            current_module_bay_id = Module.objects.filter(pk=self.pk).values_list('module_bay_id', flat=True).first()
   314	            if self.pk is None or current_module_bay_id != self.module_bay_id:
   315	                raise ValidationError({
   316	                    'module_bay': _("Cannot install a module in a disabled module bay.")
   317	                })
   318	
   319	        # Check for recursion
   320	        module = self
   321	        module_bays = []
   322	        modules = []
   323	        while module:
   324	            module_module_bay = getattr(module, "module_bay", None)
   325	            if module.pk in modules or (module_module_bay and module_module_bay.pk in module_bays):
   326	                raise ValidationError(_("A module bay cannot belong to a module installed within it."))
   327	            modules.append(module.pk)
   328	            if module_module_bay:
   329	                module_bays.append(module_module_bay.pk)
   330	            module = module_module_bay.module if module_module_bay else None
   331	
   332	    def save(self, *args, **kwargs):
   333	        is_new = self.pk is None
   334	        old_module_bay_id = None
   335	
   336	        if not is_new:
   337	            old_module_bay_id = Module.objects.filter(pk=self.pk).values_list(
   338	                'module_bay_id', flat=True
   339	            ).first()
   340	
   341	        super().save(*args, **kwargs)
   342	
   343	        if old_module_bay_id is not None and old_module_bay_id != self.module_bay_id:
   344	            for child_bay in self.modulebays.select_related('module__module_bay'):
   345	                child_bay.snapshot()
   346	                child_bay.save()
   347	
   348	        adopt_components = getattr(self, '_adopt_components', False)
   349	        disable_replication = getattr(self, '_disable_replication', False)
   350	
   351	        # We skip adding components if the module is being edited or
   352	        # both replication and component adoption is disabled
   353	        if not is_new or (disable_replication and not adopt_components):
   354	            return
   355	
   356	        # Iterate all component types
   357	        for templates, component_attribute, component_model in [
   358	            ("consoleporttemplates", "consoleports", ConsolePort),
   359	            ("consoleserverporttemplates", "consoleserverports", ConsoleServerPort),
   360	            ("interfacetemplates", "interfaces", Interface),
   361	            ("powerporttemplates", "powerports", PowerPort),
   362	            ("poweroutlettemplates", "poweroutlets", PowerOutlet),
   363	            ("rearporttemplates", "rearports", RearPort),
   364	            ("frontporttemplates", "frontports", FrontPort),
   365	            ("modulebaytemplates", "modulebays", ModuleBay),
   366	        ]:
   367	            create_instances = []
   368	            update_instances = []
   369	
   370	            # Prefetch installed components
   371	            installed_components = {
   372	                component.name: component
   373	                for component in getattr(self.device, component_attribute).filter(module__isnull=True)
   374	            }
   375	
   376	            # Get the template for the module type.
   377	            for template in getattr(self.module_type, templates).all():
   378	                template_instance = template.instantiate(device=self.device, module=self)
   379	
   380	                if adopt_components:
   381	                    existing_item = installed_components.get(template_instance.name)
   382	
   383	                    # Check if there's a component with the same name already
   384	                    if existing_item:
   385	                        # Assign it to the module
   386	                        existing_item.module = self
   387	                        update_instances.append(existing_item)
   388	                        continue
   389	
   390	                # Only create new components if replication is enabled
   391	                if not disable_replication:
   392	                    create_instances.append(template_instance)
   393	
   394	            # Set default values for any applicable custom fields
   395	            if cf_defaults := CustomField.objects.get_defaults_for_model(component_model):
   396	                for component in create_instances:
   397	                    component.custom_field_data = cf_defaults
   398	
   399	            # Set denormalized references
   400	            for component in create_instances:
   401	                component._site = self.device.site
   402	                component._location = self.device.location
   403	                component._rack = self.device.rack
   404	
   405	            # we handle create and update separately - this is for create
   406	            if not issubclass(component_model, MPTTModel):
   407	                component_model.objects.bulk_create(create_instances)
   408	                # Emit the post_save signal for each newly created object
   409	                for component in create_instances:
   410	                    post_save.send(
   411	                        sender=component_model,
   412	                        instance=component,
   413	                        created=True,
   414	                        raw=False,
   415	                        using='default',
   416	                        update_fields=None
   417	                    )
   418	            else:
   419	                # MPTT models must be saved individually to maintain tree structure
   420	                for instance in create_instances:
   421	                    instance.save()
   422	
   423	            update_fields = ['module']
   424	
   425	            # we handle create and update separately - this is for update
   426	            component_model.objects.bulk_update(update_instances, update_fields)
   427	            # Emit the post_save signal for each updated object
   428	            for component in update_instances:
   429	                post_save.send(
   430	                    sender=component_model,
   431	                    instance=component,
   432	                    created=False,
   433	                    raw=False,
   434	                    using='default',
   435	                    update_fields=update_fields
   436	                )
   437	
   438	            # Rebuild MPTT tree if needed (bulk_update bypasses model save)
   439	            if issubclass(component_model, MPTTModel) and update_instances:
   440	                component_model.objects.rebuild()
   441	
   442	        # Replicate any front/rear port mappings from the ModuleType
   443	        create_port_mappings(self.device, self.module_type, self)
   444	
   445	        # Interface bridges have to be set after interface instantiation
   446	        update_interface_bridges(self.device, self.module_type.interfacetemplates, self)

   285	    def __str__(self):
   286	        return self.name
   287	
   288	    def clean(self):
   289	        super().clean()
   290	
   291	        # Must be assigned to a site, cluster, and/or device
   292	        if not self.site and not self.cluster and not self.device:
   293	            raise ValidationError(
   294	                _('A virtual machine must be assigned to a site, cluster, or device.')
   295	            )
   296	
   297	        # Validate site for cluster & VM
   298	        if self.cluster and self.site and self.cluster._site and self.cluster._site != self.site:
   299	            raise ValidationError({
   300	                'cluster': _(
   301	                    'The selected cluster ({cluster}) is not assigned to this site ({site}).'
   302	                ).format(cluster=self.cluster, site=self.site)
   303	            })
   304	
   305	        # Validate site for the device & VM (when the device is standalone)
   306	        if self.device and self.site and self.device.site and self.device.site != self.site:
   307	            raise ValidationError({
   308	                'site': _(
   309	                    'The selected device ({device}) is not assigned to this site ({site}).'
   310	                ).format(device=self.device, site=self.site)
   311	            })
   312	
   313	        # Direct device assignment is only for standalone hosts. If the selected
   314	        # device already belongs to a cluster, require that cluster explicitly.
   315	        if self.device and not self.cluster and self.device.cluster:
   316	            raise ValidationError({
   317	                'cluster': _(
   318	                    "Must specify the assigned device's cluster ({cluster}) when assigning host device {device}."
   319	                ).format(cluster=self.device.cluster, device=self.device)
   320	            })
   321	
   322	        # Validate assigned cluster device
   323	        if self.device and self.cluster and self.device.cluster_id != self.cluster_id:
   324	            raise ValidationError({
   325	                'device': _(
   326	                    "The selected device ({device}) is not assigned to this cluster ({cluster})."
   327	                ).format(device=self.device, cluster=self.cluster)
   328	            })
   329	
   330	        # Validate aggregate disk size
   331	        if not self._state.adding:
   332	            total_disk = self.virtualdisks.aggregate(Sum('size', default=0))['size__sum']
   333	            if total_disk and self.disk is None:
   334	                self.disk = total_disk
   335	            elif total_disk and self.disk != total_disk:
   336	                raise ValidationError({
   337	                    'disk': _(
   338	                        "The specified disk size ({size}) must match the aggregate size of assigned virtual disks "
   339	                        "({total_size})."
   340	                    ).format(size=self.disk, total_size=total_disk)
   341	                })
   342	
   343	        # Validate primary IP addresses
   344	        interfaces = self.interfaces.all() if self.pk else None
   345	        for family in (4, 6):
   346	            field = f'primary_ip{family}'
   347	            ip = getattr(self, field)
   348	            if ip is not None:
   349	                if ip.address.version != family:
   350	                    raise ValidationError({
   351	                        field: _(
   352	                            "Must be an IPv{family} address. ({ip} is an IPv{version} address.)"
   353	                        ).format(family=family, ip=ip, version=ip.address.version)
   354	                    })
   355	                if ip.assigned_object in interfaces:
   356	                    pass
   357	                elif ip.nat_inside is not None and ip.nat_inside.assigned_object in interfaces:
   358	                    pass
   359	                else:
   360	                    raise ValidationError({
   361	                        field: _("The specified IP address ({ip}) is not assigned to this VM.").format(ip=ip),
   362	                    })
   363	
   364	    def save(self, *args, **kwargs):
   365	        # Assign a site from a cluster or device if not set
   366	        if not self.site:
   367	            if self.cluster and self.cluster._site:
   368	                self.site = self.cluster._site
   369	            elif self.device and self.device.site:
   370	                self.site = self.device.site
   371	
   372	        if self._state.adding:
   373	            self.apply_type_defaults()
   374	
   375	        super().save(*args, **kwargs)
   376	
   377	    def apply_type_defaults(self):
   378	        """
   379	        Populate any empty fields with defaults from the assigned VirtualMachineType.
   380	        """
   381	        if not self.virtual_machine_type_id:
   382	            return
   383	
   384	        defaults = {
   385	            'platform_id': 'default_platform_id',
   386	            'vcpus': 'default_vcpus',
   387	            'memory': 'default_memory',
   388	        }
   389	        for field, default_field in defaults.items():
   390	            if getattr(self, field) is None:
   391	                default_value = getattr(self.virtual_machine_type, default_field)
   392	                if default_value is not None:
   393	                    setattr(self, field, default_value)
   394	
   395	    def get_status_color(self):
   396	        return VirtualMachineStatusChoices.colors.get(self.status)
   397	
   398	    def get_start_on_boot_color(self):
   399	        return VirtualMachineStartOnBootChoices.colors.get(self.start_on_boot)
   400	
   401	    @property
   402	    def primary_ip(self):
   403	        if get_config().PREFER_IPV4 and self.primary_ip4:
   404	            return self.primary_ip4
   405	        if self.primary_ip6:
   406	            return self.primary_ip6
   407	        if self.primary_ip4:
   408	            return self.primary_ip4
   409	        return None

  1000	    def clean(self):

  1015	                    display_type=self.get_type_display())

  1017	            })
  1018	
  1019	        # Parent validation
  1020	
  1021	        # An interface cannot be its own parent
  1022	        if self.pk and self.parent_id == self.pk:

  1024	
  1025	        # A physical interface cannot have a parent interface

  1027	            raise ValidationError({'parent': _("Only virtual interfaces may be assigned to a parent interface.")})
  1028	
  1029	        # An interface's parent must belong to the same device or virtual chassis
  1030	        if self.parent and self.parent.device != self.device:
  1031	            if self.device.virtual_chassis is None:
  1032	                raise ValidationError({
  1033	                    'parent': _(
  1034	                        "The selected parent interface ({interface}) belongs to a different device ({device})"
  1035	                    ).format(interface=self.parent, device=self.parent.device)
  1036	                })
  1037	            if self.parent.device.virtual_chassis != self.device.virtual_chassis:
  1038	                raise ValidationError({
  1039	                    'parent': _(
  1040	                        "The selected parent interface ({interface}) belongs to {device}, which is not part of "
  1041	                        "virtual chassis {virtual_chassis}."
  1042	                    ).format(
  1043	                        interface=self.parent,
  1044	                        device=self.parent.device,
  1045	                        virtual_chassis=self.device.virtual_chassis
  1046	                    )
  1047	                })
  1048	
  1049	        # Bridge validation
  1050	
  1051	        # A bridged interface belongs to the same device or virtual chassis
  1052	        if self.bridge and self.bridge.device != self.device:
  1053	            if self.device.virtual_chassis is None:
  1054	                raise ValidationError({
  1055	                    'bridge': _(
  1056	                        "The selected bridge interface ({bridge}) belongs to a different device ({device})."
  1057	                    ).format(bridge=self.bridge, device=self.bridge.device)
  1058	                })
  1059	            if self.bridge.device.virtual_chassis != self.device.virtual_chassis:
  1060	                raise ValidationError({
  1061	                    'bridge': _(
  1062	                        "The selected bridge interface ({interface}) belongs to {device}, which is not part of virtual "
  1063	                        "chassis {virtual_chassis}."
  1064	                    ).format(
  1065	                        interface=self.bridge, device=self.bridge.device, virtual_chassis=self.device.virtual_chassis
  1066	                    )
  1067	                })
  1068	
  1069	        # LAG validation
  1070	
  1071	        # A virtual interface cannot have a parent LAG
  1072	        if self.type == InterfaceTypeChoices.TYPE_VIRTUAL and self.lag is not None:
  1073	            raise ValidationError({'lag': _("Virtual interfaces cannot have a parent LAG interface.")})
  1074	
  1075	        # A LAG interface cannot be its own parent
  1076	        if self.pk and self.lag_id == self.pk:
  1077	            raise ValidationError({'lag': _("A LAG interface cannot be its own parent.")})
  1078	
  1079	        # An interface's LAG must belong to the same device or virtual chassis
  1080	        if self.lag and self.lag.device != self.device:
  1081	            if self.device.virtual_chassis is None:
  1082	                raise ValidationError({
  1083	                    'lag': _(
  1084	                        "The selected LAG interface ({lag}) belongs to a different device ({device})."
  1085	                    ).format(lag=self.lag, device=self.lag.device)
  1086	                })
  1087	            if self.lag.device.virtual_chassis != self.device.virtual_chassis:
  1088	                raise ValidationError({

  1093	                    )
  1094	                })
  1095	
  1096	        # Wireless validation
  1097	
  1098	        # RF channel may only be set for wireless interfaces
  1099	        if self.rf_channel and not self.is_wireless:
  1100	            raise ValidationError({'rf_channel': _("Channel may be set only on wireless interfaces.")})
  1101	
  1102	        # Validate channel frequency against interface type and selected channel (if any)
  1103	        if self.rf_channel_frequency:

  1108	            if self.rf_channel and self.rf_channel_frequency != get_channel_attr(self.rf_channel, 'frequency'):

  1111	                })
  1112	
  1113	        # Validate channel width against interface type and selected channel (if any)

  1119

  1123	
  1124	        # Validate untagged VLAN

  1126	            raise ValidationError({

  1130	                ).format(untagged_vlan=self.untagged_vlan)
  1131	            })
  1132	
  1133	    def save(self, *args, **kwargs):
  1134	
  1135	        # Set absolute channel attributes from selected options

  1138	        if self.rf_channel and not self.rf_channel_width:

  1141	        super().save(*args, **kwargs)

  1143	    @property

  1145	        return super()._occupied or bool(self.wireless_link_id)
  1146	
  1147	    @property

  1149	        return not self.is_virtual and not self.is_wireless
  1150	
  1151	    @property

  1153	        return self.type in VIRTUAL_IFACE_TYPES
  1154	
  1155	    @property

  1157	        return self.type in WIRELESS_IFACE_TYPES
  1158	
  1159	    @property
  1160	    def is_lag(self):
  1161	        return self.type == InterfaceTypeChoices.TYPE_LAG
  1162	
  1163	    @property
  1164	    def is_bridge(self):
  1165	        return self.type == InterfaceTypeChoices.TYPE_BRIDGE
  1166	
  1167	    @property
  1168	    def link(self):
  1169	        return self.cable or self.wireless_link
  1170	
  1171	    @cached_property
  1172	    def link_peers(self):
  1173	        if self.cable:
  1174	            return super().link_peers
  1175	        if self.wireless_link:
  1176	            # Return the opposite side of the attached wireless link
  1177	            if self.wireless_link.interface_a == self:
  1178	                return [self.wireless_link.interface_b]
  1179	            return [self.wireless_link.interface_a]
  1180	        return []
  1181	
  1182	    @property
  1183	    def l2vpn_termination(self):
  1184	        return self.l2vpn_terminations.first()

  1187	    def connected_endpoints(self):
  1188	        # If this is a virtual interface, return the remote endpoint of the connected
  1189	        # virtual circuit, if any.

   144	                        ).format(count=nonsite_devices, site=site)
   145	                    })
   146	            if location:
   147	                if nonlocation_devices := Device.objects.filter(cluster=self).exclude(location=location).count():
   148	                    raise ValidationError({
   149	                        'scope': _(
   150	                            "{count} devices are assigned as hosts for this cluster but are not in location {location}"
   151	                        ).format(count=nonlocation_devices, location=location)
   152	                    })

At this point the contract shape is clear: `Device` owns identity/location/rack/IP/cluster/VC fields, and several other models either FK to it directly or denormalize it for filtering. I’m collecting the UI/API/filter surfaces now, because those are the loud break points if field names, lookup semantics, or nested serializers change.

   184	class ModuleSerializer(PrimaryModelSerializer):
   185	    device = DeviceSerializer(nested=True)
   186	    module_bay = NestedModuleBaySerializer()
   187	    module_type = ModuleTypeSerializer(nested=True)
   188	    status = ChoiceField(choices=ModuleStatusChoices, required=False)
   189	    replicate_components = serializers.BooleanField(
   190	        required=False,
   191	        default=True,
   192	        write_only=True,
   193	        label=_('Replicate components'),
   194	        help_text=_('Automatically populate components associated with this module type (default: true)')
   195	    )
   196	    adopt_components = serializers.BooleanField(
   197	        required=False,
   198	        default=False,
   199	        write_only=True,
   200	        label=_('Adopt components'),
   201	        help_text=_('Adopt already existing components')
   202	    )
   203	
   204	    class Meta:
   205	        model = Module
   206	        fields = [
   207	            'id', 'url', 'display_url', 'display', 'device', 'module_bay', 'module_type', 'status', 'serial',
   208	            'asset_tag', 'description', 'owner', 'comments', 'tags', 'custom_fields', 'created', 'last_updated',
   209	            'replicate_components', 'adopt_components',
   210	        ]
   211	        brief_fields = ('id', 'url', 'display', 'device', 'module_bay', 'module_type', 'description')
   212	
   213	    def validate(self, data):
   214	        # When used as a nested serializer (e.g. as the `module` field on device component
   215	        # serializers), `data` is already a resolved Module instance — skip our custom logic.
   216	        if self.nested:
   217	            return super().validate(data)
   218	
   219	        # Pop write-only transient fields before ValidatedModelSerializer tries to
   220	        # construct a Module instance for full_clean(); restore them afterwards.
   221	        replicate_components = data.pop('replicate_components', True)
   222	        adopt_components = data.pop('adopt_components', False)
   223	        data = super().validate(data)
   224	
   225	        # For updates these fields are not meaningful; omit them from validated_data so that
   226	        # ModelSerializer.update() does not set unexpected attributes on the instance.
   227	        if self.instance:
   228	            return data
   229	
   230	        # Always pass the flags to create() so it can set the correct private attributes.
   231	        data['replicate_components'] = replicate_components
   232	        data['adopt_components'] = adopt_components
   233	
   234	        # Skip conflict checks when no component operations are requested.
   235	        if not replicate_components and not adopt_components:
   236	            return data
   237	
   238	        device = data.get('device')
   239	        module_type = data.get('module_type')
   240	        module_bay = data.get('module_bay')
   241	
   242	        # Required-field validation fires separately; skip here if any are missing.
   243	        if not all([device, module_type, module_bay]):
   244	            return data

   272	    def validate(self, data):
   273	
   274	        if not self.nested:
   275	
   276	            # Validate 802.1q mode and vlan(s)
   277	            mode = None
   278	            tagged_vlans = []
   279	
   280	            # Gather Information
   281	            if self.instance:
   282	                mode = data.get('mode') if 'mode' in data.keys() else self.instance.mode
   283	                untagged_vlan = data.get('untagged_vlan') if 'untagged_vlan' in data.keys() else \
   284	                    self.instance.untagged_vlan
   285	                qinq_svlan = data.get('qinq_svlan') if 'qinq_svlan' in data.keys() else \
   286	                    self.instance.qinq_svlan
   287	                tagged_vlans = data.get('tagged_vlans') if 'tagged_vlans' in data.keys() else \
   288	                    self.instance.tagged_vlans.all()
   289	            else:
   290	                mode = data.get('mode', None)
   291	                untagged_vlan = data.get('untagged_vlan') if 'untagged_vlan' in data.keys() else None
   292	                qinq_svlan = data.get('qinq_svlan') if 'qinq_svlan' in data.keys() else None
   293	                tagged_vlans = data.get('tagged_vlans') if 'tagged_vlans' in data.keys() else None
   294	
   295	            errors = {}
   296	
   297	            # Non Q-in-Q mode with service vlan set
   298	            if mode != InterfaceModeChoices.MODE_Q_IN_Q and qinq_svlan:
   299	                errors.update({
   300	                    'qinq_svlan': _("Interface mode does not support q-in-q service vlan")
   301	                })
   302	            # Routed mode
   303	            if not mode:
   304	                # Untagged vlan
   305	                if untagged_vlan:
   306	                    errors.update({
   307	                        'untagged_vlan': _("Interface mode does not support untagged vlan")
   308	                    })
   309	                # Tagged vlan
   310	                if tagged_vlans:
   311	                    errors.update({
   312	                        'tagged_vlans': _("Interface mode does not support tagged vlans")
   313	                    })
   314	            # Non-tagged mode
   315	            elif mode in (InterfaceModeChoices.MODE_TAGGED_ALL, InterfaceModeChoices.MODE_ACCESS) and tagged_vlans:
   316	                errors.update({
   317	                    'tagged_vlans': _("Interface mode does not support tagged vlans")
   318	                })
   319	
   320	            if errors:
   321	                raise serializers.ValidationError(errors)
   322	
   323	            # Validate many-to-many VLAN assignments
   324	            device = self.instance.device if self.instance else data.get('device')
   325	            for vlan in data.get('tagged_vlans', []):
   326	                if vlan.site not in [device.site, None]:
   327	                    raise serializers.ValidationError({
   328	                        'tagged_vlans': f"VLAN {vlan} must belong to the same site as the interface's parent device, "
   329	                                        f"or it must be global."
   330	                    })
   331	
   332	        return super().validate(data)

   426	    def get_serializer_class(self):
   427	        """
   428	        Select the specific serializer based on the request context.
   429	
   430	        If the `brief` query param equates to True, return the NestedDeviceSerializer
   431	
   432	        If the `exclude` query param includes `config_context` as a value, return the DeviceSerializer
   433	
   434	        Else, return the DeviceWithConfigContextSerializer
   435	        """
   436	        request = self.get_serializer_context()['request']
   437	        if self.brief or 'config_context' in request.query_params.get('exclude', []):
   438	            return serializers.DeviceSerializer
   439	
   440	        return serializers.DeviceWithConfigContextSerializer

   636	class ConnectedDeviceViewSet(ViewSet):
   637	    """
   638	    This endpoint allows a user to determine what device (if any) is connected to a given peer device and peer
   639	    interface. This is useful in a situation where a device boots with no configuration, but can detect its neighbors
   640	    via a protocol such as LLDP. Two query parameters must be included in the request:
   641	
   642	    * `peer_device`: The name of the peer device
   643	    * `peer_interface`: The name of the peer interface
   644	    """
   645	    permission_classes = [IsAuthenticatedOrLoginNotRequired]
   646	    _device_param = OpenApiParameter(
   647	        name='peer_device',
   648	        location='query',
   649	        description='The name of the peer device',
   650	        required=True,
   651	        type=OpenApiTypes.STR
   652	    )
   653	    _interface_param = OpenApiParameter(
   654	        name='peer_interface',
   655	        location='query',
   656	        description='The name of the peer interface',
   657	        required=True,
   658	        type=OpenApiTypes.STR
   659	    )
   660	    serializer_class = serializers.DeviceSerializer
   661	
   662	    def get_view_name(self):
   663	        return "Connected Device Locator"
   664	
   665	    @extend_schema(
   666	        parameters=[_device_param, _interface_param],
   667	        responses={200: serializers.DeviceSerializer}
   668	    )
   669	    def list(self, request):
   670	
   671	        peer_device_name = request.query_params.get(self._device_param.name)
   672	        peer_interface_name = request.query_params.get(self._interface_param.name)
   673	
   674	        if not peer_device_name or not peer_interface_name:
   675	            raise MissingFilterException(detail='Request must include "peer_device" and "peer_interface" filters.')
   676	
   677	        # Determine local endpoint from peer interface's connection
   678	        peer_device = get_object_or_404(
   679	            Device.objects.restrict(request.user, 'view'),
   680	            name=peer_device_name
   681	        )
   682	        peer_interface = get_object_or_404(
   683	            Interface.objects.restrict(request.user, 'view'),
   684	            device=peer_device,
   685	            name=peer_interface_name
   686	        )
   687	        endpoints = peer_interface.connected_endpoints
   688	
   689	        # If an Interface, return the parent device
   690	        if endpoints and type(endpoints[0]) is Interface:
   691	            device = get_object_or_404(
   692	                Device.objects.restrict(request.user, 'view'),
   693	                pk=endpoints[0].device_id
   694	            )
   695	            return Response(serializers.DeviceSerializer(device, context={'request': request}).data)
   696	
   697	        # Connected endpoint is none or not an Interface
   698	        raise Http404

  1484	    def search(self, queryset, name, value):
  1485	        if not value.strip():
  1486	            return queryset
  1487	        qs_filter = (
  1488	            Q(name__icontains=value) |
  1489	            Q(virtual_chassis__name__icontains=value) |
  1490	            Q(serial__icontains=value.strip()) |
  1491	            Q(asset_tag__icontains=value.strip()) |
  1492	            Q(description__icontains=value.strip()) |
  1493	            Q(comments__icontains=value)
  1494	        )
  1495	        # If the given value looks like an IP address, look for primary IPv4/IPv6 assignments
  1496	        try:
  1497	            ipaddress = netaddr.IPNetwork(value)
  1498	            if ipaddress.version == 4:
  1499	                qs_filter |= Q(primary_ip4__address__host__inet=ipaddress.ip)
  1500	            elif ipaddress.version == 6:
  1501	                qs_filter |= Q(primary_ip6__address__host__inet=ipaddress.ip)
  1502	        except (AddrFormatError, ValueError):
  1503	            pass
  1504	        return queryset.filter(qs_filter)
  1505	
  1506	    def _has_primary_ip(self, queryset, name, value):
  1507	        params = Q(primary_ip4__isnull=False) | Q(primary_ip6__isnull=False)
  1508	        if value:
  1509	            return queryset.filter(params)
  1510	        return queryset.exclude(params)
  1511

  1513	        params = Q(oob_ip__isnull=False)
  1514	        if value:
  1515	            return queryset.filter(params)
  1516	        return queryset.exclude(params)

  2087	    def search(self, queryset, name, value):
  2088	        if not value.strip():
  2089	            return queryset
  2090	        qs_filter = (
  2091	            Q(mac_address__icontains=value) |
  2092	            Q(description__icontains=value)
  2093	        )
  2094	        return queryset.filter(qs_filter)
  2095	
  2096	    def filter_device(self, queryset, name, value):
  2097	        devices = Device.objects.filter(**{f'{name}__in': value})
  2098	        if not devices.exists():
  2099	            return queryset.none()
  2100	        interface_ids = []
  2101	        for device in devices:
  2102	            interface_ids.extend(device.vc_interfaces().values_list('id', flat=True))
  2103	        return queryset.filter(
  2104	            interface__in=interface_ids
  2105	        )
  2106	
  2107	    def filter_virtual_machine(self, queryset, name, value):
  2108	        virtual_machines = VirtualMachine.objects.filter(**{f'{name}__in': value})
  2109	        if not virtual_machines.exists():
  2110	            return queryset.none()
  2111	        interface_ids = []
  2112	        for vm in virtual_machines:
  2113	            interface_ids.extend(vm.interfaces.values_list('id', flat=True))
  2114	        return queryset.filter(
  2115	            vminterface__in=interface_ids
  2116	        )
  2117	
  2118	    def filter_assigned(self, queryset, name, value):
  2119	        params = {
  2120	            'assigned_object_type__isnull': True,
  2121	            'assigned_object_id__isnull': True,
  2122	        }
  2123	        if value:
  2124	            return queryset.exclude(**params)
  2125	        return queryset.filter(**params)
  2126	
  2127	    def filter_primary(self, queryset, name, value):
  2128	        interface_mac_ids = Interface.objects.filter(primary_mac_address_id__isnull=False).values_list(
  2129	            'primary_mac_address_id', flat=True
  2130	        )
  2131	        vminterface_mac_ids = VMInterface.objects.filter(primary_mac_address_id__isnull=False).values_list(
  2132	            'primary_mac_address_id', flat=True
  2133	        )
  2134	        query = Q(pk__in=interface_mac_ids) | Q(pk__in=vminterface_mac_ids)
  2135	        if value:
  2136	            return queryset.filter(query)
  2137	        return queryset.exclude(query)

    58	@receiver(post_save, sender=Location)
    59	def handle_location_site_change(instance, created, **kwargs):
    60	    """
    61	    Update child objects if Site assignment has changed. We intentionally recurse through each child
    62	    object instead of calling update() on the QuerySet to ensure the proper change records get created for each.
    63	    """
    64	    if not created:
    65	        instance.get_descendants().update(site=instance.site)
    66	        locations = instance.get_descendants(include_self=True).values_list('pk', flat=True)
    67	        Rack.objects.filter(location__in=locations).update(site=instance.site)
    68	        Device.objects.filter(location__in=locations).update(site=instance.site)
    69	        PowerPanel.objects.filter(location__in=locations).update(site=instance.site)
    70	        CableTermination.objects.filter(_location__in=locations).update(_site=instance.site)
    71	        # Update component models for devices in these locations
    72	        for model in COMPONENT_MODELS:
    73	            model.objects.filter(device__location__in=locations).update(_site=instance.site)
    74	
    75	
    76	@receiver(post_save, sender=Rack)
    77	def handle_rack_site_change(instance, created, **kwargs):
    78	    """
    79	    Update child Devices if Site or Location assignment has changed.
    80	    """
    81	    if not created:
    82	        Device.objects.filter(rack=instance).update(site=instance.site, location=instance.location)
    83	        # Update component models for devices in this rack
    84	        for model in COMPONENT_MODELS:
    85	            model.objects.filter(device__rack=instance).update(
    86	                _site=instance.site,
    87	                _location=instance.location,
    88	            )
    89	
    90	
    91	@receiver(post_save, sender=Device)
    92	def handle_device_site_change(instance, created, **kwargs):
    93	    """
    94	    Update child components to update the parent Site, Location, and Rack when a Device is saved.
    95	    """
    96	    if not created:
    97	        for model in COMPONENT_MODELS:
    98	            model.objects.filter(device=instance).update(
    99	                _site=instance.site,
   100	                _location=instance.location,
   101	                _rack=instance.rack,
   102	            )

   109	@receiver(post_save, sender=VirtualChassis)
   110	def assign_virtualchassis_master(instance, created, **kwargs):
   111	    """
   112	    When a VirtualChassis is created, automatically assign its master device (if any) to the VC.
   113	    """
   114	    if created and instance.master:
   115	        master = Device.objects.get(pk=instance.master.pk)
   116	        master.virtual_chassis = instance
   117	        master.vc_position = 1
   118	        master.save()

   121	@receiver(post_save, sender=VirtualChassis)
   122	def update_virtualchassis_member_search_cache(instance, created, raw=False, update_fields=None, **kwargs):
   123	    """
   124	    Refresh the search cache for member Devices when a VirtualChassis is renamed. DeviceIndex caches
   125	    virtual_chassis as its string value, so a rename would otherwise leave stale CachedValue entries.
   126	    """
   127	    if raw or created:
   128	        return
   129	    # The VC name is the only VC attribute cached on member Devices; skip saves that can't change it.
   130	    if update_fields is not None and 'name' not in update_fields:
   131	        return
   132	    search_backend.cache(
   133	        Device.objects.filter(virtual_chassis=instance).select_related('virtual_chassis'),
   134	        indexer=DeviceIndex,
   135	        remove_existing=True
   136	    )

   731	    def search(self, queryset, name, value):
   732	        if not value.strip():
   733	            return queryset
   734	        qs_filter = (
   735	            Q(dns_name__icontains=value) |
   736	            Q(description__icontains=value) |
   737	            Q(address__istartswith=value)
   738	        )
   739	        return queryset.filter(qs_filter)
   740	
   741	    def search_by_parent(self, queryset, name, value):
   742	        if not value:
   743	            return queryset
   744	        q = Q()
   745	        for prefix in value:
   746	            try:
   747	                query = str(netaddr.IPNetwork(prefix.strip()).cidr)
   748	                q |= Q(address__net_host_contained=query)
   749	            except (AddrFormatError, ValueError):
   750	                return queryset.none()
   751	        return queryset.filter(q)
   752	
   753	    def parse_inet_addresses(self, value):
   754	        """
   755	        Parse networks or IP addresses and cast to a format
   756	        acceptable by the Postgres inet type.
   757	
   758	        Skips invalid values.
   759	        """
   760	        parsed = []
   761	        for addr in value:
   762	            if netaddr.valid_ipv4(addr) or netaddr.valid_ipv6(addr):
   763	                parsed.append(addr)
   764	                continue
   765	            try:
   766	                network = netaddr.IPNetwork(addr)
   767	                parsed.append(str(network))
   768	            except (AddrFormatError, ValueError):
   769	                continue
   770	        return parsed
   771	
   772	    def filter_address(self, queryset, name, value):
   773	        # Let's first parse the addresses passed
   774	        # as argument. If they are all invalid,
   775	        # we return an empty queryset
   776	        value = self.parse_inet_addresses(value)
   777	        if len(value) == 0:
   778	            return queryset.none()
   779	
   780	        try:
   781	            return queryset.filter(address__net_in=value)
   782	        except ValidationError:
   783	            return queryset.none()
   784	
   785	    @extend_schema_field(OpenApiTypes.STR)
   786	    def filter_present_in_vrf(self, queryset, name, vrf):
   787	        if vrf is None:
   788	            return queryset.none()
   789	        return queryset.filter(
   790	            Q(vrf=vrf) |
   791	            Q(vrf__export_targets__in=vrf.import_targets.all())
   792	        ).distinct()
   793	
   794	    def filter_device(self, queryset, name, value):
   795	        devices = Device.objects.filter(**{'{}__in'.format(name): value})
   796	        if not devices.exists():
   797	            return queryset.none()
   798	        interface_ids = []
   799	        for device in devices:
   800	            interface_ids.extend(device.vc_interfaces().values_list('id', flat=True))
   801	        return queryset.filter(
   802	            interface__in=interface_ids
   803	        )

   806	        virtual_machines = VirtualMachine.objects.filter(**{'{}__in'.format(name): value})

   809	        interface_ids = []

   812	        return queryset.filter(

   814	        )

   817	        content_types = ContentType.objects.get_for_models(Interface, VMInterface).values()
   818	        if value:
   819	            return queryset.filter(
   820	                assigned_object_type__in=content_types,

   822	            )
   823	        return queryset.exclude(

   826	        )
   827	
   828	    def _assigned(self, queryset, name, value):

   834	        return queryset.filter(

   868	    @extend_schema_field(OpenApiTypes.STR)
   869	    def filter_related_ip(self, queryset, name, value):
   870	        """
   871	        Filter by VRF & prefix of assigned IP addresses.
   872	        """
   873	        ip_filter = Q()
   874	        for ipaddress in value:
   875	            if ipaddress.vrf:
   876	                q = Q(
   877	                    ip_addresses__address__net_contained_or_equal=ipaddress.address,
   878	                    ip_addresses__vrf=ipaddress.vrf
   879	                )
   880	            else:
   881	                q = Q(
   882	                    ip_addresses__address__net_contained_or_equal=ipaddress.address,
   883	                    ip_addresses__vrf__isnull=True
   884	                )
   885	            ip_filter |= q
   886	
   887	        return queryset.filter(ip_filter)
